summary: Tested /celebrate-soul CelebrateCategoryStrip and CelebrateContentModal fixes. All backend APIs pass. Frontend: all 11 categories found, modal opens, products load with object-contain. Critical bug: desktop modal centering broken (Framer Motion overrides CSS transform). Admin panel: 415 products load, native category select with 19+ options works.

ui_bugs.issue: Desktop modal NOT centered. Modal appears on the right half of the screen starting at x=960 (left: 50% of 1920px viewport) instead of being centered. getBoundingClientRect shows rectLeft=960, center_x=1530 vs expected 960. Computed CSS transform is 'matrix(1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 54.9)' - only Y-translation, no X-centering.
ui_bugs.root_cause: Framer Motion's scale animation (initial={scale:0.96} → animate={scale:1}) generates its own CSS transform matrix that REPLACES the inline style transform:'translate(-50%, -50%)'. Framer Motion takes ownership of the CSS transform property, discarding the centering transform.
ui_bugs.fix:
Option A (recommended): Wrap desktop motion.div in a centering wrapper:
...
. Option B: Use Framer Motion's x/y with percentage strings: initial={{scale:0.96, x:'-50%', y:'-50%'}} animate={{scale:1, x:'-50%', y:'-50%'}} and remove transform from inline style.

issues: Mobile: category strip click on Birthday Cakes did not open modal in mobile viewport test (possibly scroll-position issue in test, needs further verification)

action_items: FIX (HIGH): CelebrateContentModal desktop centering broken - Framer Motion scale animation overrides transform:translate(-50%,-50%). Fix by using wrapper div flexbox centering OR Framer Motion x/y percentage values. See ui_bugs[0].fix for exact fix.
VERIFY: Test mobile category strip click - modal may not open on mobile (could be scroll-related in automated test, manual verification recommended)
critical_code_review_comments: CelebrateContentModal.jsx lines 452-473: modal centering uses CSS transform which Framer Motion overrides. Known Framer Motion issue. Use wrapper div pattern (same as ProductDetailModal at ProductCard.jsx:1097 which uses 'fixed inset-0 flex items-center justify-center' correctly).

CelebrateContentModal.jsx: Mobile modal renders ALL products from desktop rendering path (category !== 'bundles' && category !== 'soul-picks') but mobile modal doesn't have full product grid rendering. However this is acceptable since mobile gets bottom-sheet with products via the same component (the isDesktop check handles this).
ProductCard.jsx line 438: object-contain confirmed ✅, line 1116: object-contain confirmed ✅
CelebrateManager.jsx: limit=2000 returns 415 celebrate products ✅, native select category dropdown ✅

success_rate.frontend: 85% (11/11 categories shown, modal opens, products load, images contain - only desktop centering broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: CelebrateCategoryStrip (11 categories) and CelebrateContentModal are working. Main remaining bug: desktop modal centering (transform conflict with Framer Motion scale animation). Admin panel works at /admin (login: aditya/lola4304), click '14 PILLARS > Celebrate' (data-testid='admin-tab-products') to access CelebrateManager. Backend APIs all working. After fixing modal centering, verify with data-testid='celebrate-modal-birthday-cakes' and check getBoundingClientRect().left is ~390px (not 960px) in 1920px viewport.

rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Login as dipali@clubconcierge.in, 2. Navigate to /celebrate-soul, 3. Click 'Birthday Cakes' category, 4. Modal opens at x=960 (right half of 1920px viewport) instead of centered at x=390. getBoundingClientRect shows left=960, width=1140, center_x=1530 vs expected 960.
rca_of_issue.root_cause: CelebrateContentModal.jsx line 456: style includes transform:'translate(-50%,-50%)' for centering. motion.div uses Framer Motion animate={scale:0.96→scale:1}. Framer Motion's animation system computes its own CSS transform matrix for the scale animation, which REPLACES the inline style transform property. Computed CSS: 'matrix(1,0,0,1,0,54.9)' - only Y offset from spring animation, no X translation.
rca_of_issue.fix:
Remove transform from inline style. Use wrapper div approach:
content
. This is the same pattern as ProductDetailModal (ProductCard.jsx line 1097) which works correctly.
